ogTitle: McMinns Lagoon vs Mereenie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McMinns Lagoon and Mereenie.
heading: McMinns Lagoon vs Mereenie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McMinns Lagoon, Northern Territory and Mereenie, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mereenie has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to McMinns Lagoon (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McMinns Lagoon or Mereenie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McMinns Lagoon has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Mereenie scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McMinns Lagoon scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Mereenie scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McMinns Lagoon or Mereenie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mereenie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
